context: Items where Gemini passes disagreed. Review and classify.

items.text: Colorado Springs. The high desert of the American West. Eighteen ninety-nine.
items.gemini_reasoning: Independently verifiable historical location and date for Tesla's activities.

items.text: Tesla moves his laboratory to the high desert.
items.gemini_reasoning: Tesla indeed established a laboratory in Colorado Springs.

items.text: He claims he needs the altitude. He claims he needs the dry air.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Tesla's stated beliefs or justifications for his choice of location.

items.text: He sets up his station on the shadow line of Pikes Peak, in a geological zone known for intense piezoelectric activity.
items.gemini_reasoning: Pikes Peak and piezoelectric activity are verifiable concepts; Tesla's choice of location near Pikes Peak is historical, though the specific 'shadow line' implication is presented within the narrative.

items.text: He builds a magnifying transmitter. A coil fifty feet in diameter.
items.gemini_reasoning: Tesla is historically known for building magnifying transmitters, and the general scale described is plausible for his experiments.

items.text: He tells the press he is going to send a radio signal to Paris. He tells his financiers he is going to send power to London without wires.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Tesla's stated intentions and claims to external parties.

items.text: the Black Notebook
items.gemini_reasoning: Introduced as a specific, significant artifact within the narrative that is distinct from widely known historical records, indicating it's part of the series' invented lore.

items.text: the famous Colorado Springs Notes that were published in the nineteen seventies.
items.gemini_reasoning: Refers to actual historical documents and their publication.

items.text: This is a separate volume. A small, water-damaged ledger that was found in the pocket of his coat after the laboratory was dismantled.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes the Black Notebook as a distinct, previously unknown artifact, details of its discovery are fictionalized within the documentary's lore.

items.text: On page forty-two, there is a drawing. It is a map of the stars.
items.gemini_reasoning: A specific, non-verifiable detail about the contents of the Black Notebook, central to the narrative's lore.

items.text: Tesla was an obsessive observer of the night sky. He knew the constellations. He knew the position of Polaris.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Tesla's character traits and knowledge as presented in the narrative.

items.text: the sky he drew in that notebook does not match the sky over Colorado. The constellations are recognizable, but they are distorted. The angles are wrong. Orion's belt is tilted fifteen degrees too far to the south. The Pleiades are compressed.
items.gemini_reasoning: Detailed, specific alterations to a known reality (the sky over Colorado), presented as fact within the lore of the Black Notebook's contents.

items.text: And there is a star marked in the northern quadrant that does not exist in our catalogs. He labeled it Dom... Home.
items.gemini_reasoning: A key piece of invented lore: a non-existent star labeled 'Home' found in a hidden notebook.

items.text: He told the world he was sending power to London.
items.gemini_reasoning: Restatement of Tesla's public claims/intentions.

items.text: the earth fought him
items.gemini_reasoning: Personification of the earth, creating a distinct sensory image of conflict.

items.text: On the night of July third, eighteen ninety-nine, Tesla ordered his assistant to close the switch.
items.gemini_reasoning: A specific date and an action historically associated with Tesla's Colorado Springs experiments, though the subsequent events are lore.

items.text: The magnifying transmitter began to pump millions of volts into the earth's crust. It created a standing wave of energy that circled the globe, bouncing off the antipodes and returning to the source.
items.gemini_reasoning: Detailed and scientifically speculative events attributed to the magnifying transmitter, presented as factual within the documentary's invented lore.

items.text: The official record says the local power station caught fire. It says the dynamo burned out because of the back-feed.
items.gemini_reasoning: Refers to a historically recorded event and its official explanation, even if the narrative later offers alternative perspectives.

items.text: But look at the reports from the townspeople of Colorado Springs. They didn't just see sparks.
items.gemini_reasoning: Introduces an alternative, unofficial account from 'townspeople' that contradicts or expands upon the official record, typical of lore-building.

items.text: A rancher riding three miles out of town, reported that the barbed wire fences began to glow. Not with heat, but with a cold, blue light. He said the light was liquid. He said it dripped off the wire and pooled on the ground, but did not burn the grass.
items.gemini_reasoning: A highly descriptive, fantastical event witnessed by a fictional 'rancher,' clearly part of the invented lore.

items.text: the light was liquid. He said it dripped off the wire and pooled on the ground
items.gemini_reasoning: A vivid and unusual metaphor comparing light to a liquid, creating a strong sensory image.

items.text: In the town center, people walking on the wooden sidewalks felt the nails in their shoes heat up. But they also reported something else. They reported that their shadows detached.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes unusual, non-verifiable phenomena (heating nails, detached shadows) witnessed by townspeople, central to the narrative's lore.

items.text: Three separate witnesses, a schoolteacher, a bartender, and the local sheriff, gave statements that night. They said that as the humming from the laboratory grew louder, the shadows cast by the gas lamps stopped moving in sync with the people who cast them. The shadows lagged. They stuttered.
items.gemini_reasoning: Detailed accounts from fictional witnesses describing surreal, non-verifiable phenomena (detached, lagging shadows), reinforcing the invented lore.

items.text: The shadows lagged. They stuttered.
items.gemini_reasoning: Personification of shadows, creating a distinct and unsettling visual/behavioral description.

items.text: One witness said his shadow raised its hand a full second before he did.
items.gemini_reasoning: A specific, surreal detail from a fictional witness, deepening the invented lore of the detached shadows.

items.text: Tesla wrote in his diary that night. The resonance is established. The barrier is thin. I can hear them breathing.
items.gemini_reasoning: A fictional diary entry by Tesla, revealing his internal experience of the lore events.

items.text: The resonance is established. The barrier is thin. I can hear them breathing.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Tesla's subjective experience and belief during the event.

items.text: The barrier is thin. I can hear them breathing.
items.gemini_reasoning: Evocative language describing a mystical or supernatural experience.

items.text: He claimed he heard signals from Mars. Rhythmic counting. One. Two. Three.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Tesla's claim about the source and nature of the signals he heard.

items.text: The frequencies he detected were earth-bound???propagating through the ground, not through space.
items.gemini_reasoning: Presented as a 'factual' correction within the narrative, indicating the mysterious signals are local, not extraterrestrial, thereby building lore.

items.text: Healthy timeline trying to contact the infection
items.gemini_reasoning: These are introduced as specific, abstract concepts within the narrative's invented lore, integral to the 'open mystery'.

items.text: He burned the papers from that night. He packed up the lab. He never spoke of the counting again.
items.gemini_reasoning: Fictional actions attributed to Tesla to conceal or suppress the events of that night, central to the documentary's lore.

items.text: three weeks later, he filed a patent for a receiver for extra-terrestrial signals.
items.gemini_reasoning: Tesla is historically known to have filed such patents. The specific timing and implied motivation are fictionalized context within the lore.

items.text: The patent office rejected it. They said it was impossible.
items.gemini_reasoning: A historically verifiable outcome for some of Tesla's more speculative patent applications.

items.text: In this physics, it is impossible. But Tesla wasn't working in this physics.
items.gemini_reasoning: Establishes a core lore concept about the existence of different 'physics' or realities that Tesla might have been operating within.
